title: noronhia coriacea
intro: Noronhia coriacea, also known as the Madagascar ironwood or Traveller's palm, is a plant species that belongs to the family Ochnaceae and is native to the island of Madagascar. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antimalarial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties, which can help in treating various health issues. The health benefits of Noronhia coriacea include reducing fever, relieving pain and inflammation, and aiding in wound healing. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and its bark can be used to make a medicinal decoction.
